_meta.purpose: Full blood count (CBC) + indices reference ranges -> merged into reference_ranges.RANGES (add-only; tested defaults win) and searchable KB. Adult ranges; apply age/sex + local validation. Decision support.
_meta.source: Dacie & Lewis Practical Haematology; typical adult ranges (validate locally).

hb.name: Haemoglobin
hb.note: Female 12.0-15.0; <7 usually needs transfusion review; >20 -> polycythaemia/dehydration.

rbc.name: Red cell count
rbc.note: Female 4.0-5.2. High RBC + low MCV -> think thalassaemia trait.

hct.name: Haematocrit / PCV
hct.note: Female 36-48. Hct >55% falsely prolongs coag (citrate ratio).

mcv.name: Mean cell volume
mcv.note: <80 microcytic (iron def / thalassaemia / ACD); >100 macrocytic (B12/folate, alcohol, hypothyroid, MDS).

mchc.name: Mean cell Hb concentration
mchc.note: High MCHC -> spherocytosis / cold agglutinins / lipaemia artefact.

rdw.name: Red cell distribution width
rdw.note: High RDW (anisocytosis): iron deficiency vs thalassaemia (normal RDW), mixed deficiency.

wbc.name: White cell count
wbc.note: WBC <1.0 (or ANC <0.5) neutropenic sepsis risk; very high -> leukaemia, review PBS.

neutrophils.name: Neutrophils (absolute)
neutrophils.note: ANC <0.5 = severe neutropenia -> urgent, isolate, PBS.

lymphocytes.name: Lymphocytes (absolute)
lymphocytes.note: High -> viral/CLL (review PBS/flow); low -> HIV, steroids, sepsis.

eosinophils.name: Eosinophils (absolute)
eosinophils.note: High -> allergy, parasites (stool O&P), drug reaction, HES.

platelets.name: Platelets
platelets.note: <20 spontaneous bleeding risk; if low, exclude EDTA clumping (review film/citrate); >1000 -> MPN vs reactive.

mpv.name: Mean platelet volume
mpv.note: High MPV with low platelets -> peripheral destruction (ITP).

reticulocytes.name: Reticulocytes
reticulocytes.note: High -> haemolysis/blood loss response; low with anaemia -> hypoproliferative marrow.

esr.name: ESR
esr.note: Non-specific; markedly high -> infection, inflammation, myeloma, malignancy.

nrbc.name: Nucleated RBC
nrbc.note: Any nRBC in an adult is abnormal -> marrow stress/infiltration; WBC auto-count must be corrected.
